item.title: As Monday's go this was the shittiest!
item.description: Wasn't going to post about it but there is something to be said about screaming into the void! I really am having a difficult time dealing with all of it. This was not something I expected to make most viral. I just needed to get shit off my chest. Ok, so obviously I couldn't sleep a wink last night, I took all your great advice and researched my state and it turns out y'all are right. So I emailed HR and told them I didn't think their policy and my write up was legal.
item.dc:creator: Iblamemyparentstoo

item.title: For imgur15 I thought I'd reshare this absolute badass, because she deserves all the recognition possible!!

item.dc:creator: wolfwinter

item.title: Nanna's 18th birthday
item.description:
Hi everyone, I'm MrPuckett Today is my dog's 18th birthday!!!
If you know the story of Nanna you know this is the most unexpected of birthdays We were all pretty sure she would die within a couple weeks of coming home
This was Nanna in the shelter two and a half years ago Her previous owners surrendered her at 15 years old blind, deaf, terminal heart disease, incontinence, skin/ear/eye infections and arthritis so bad she could barely stand up.
So I took her home. I figured I could show her a fun couple weeks or months before she dies I prepared every meal for her from scratch, we did canine stretches every day and I just wanted her to be comfortable for once She was in so much pain.
I pushed her all over Nashville in a stroller and slowly started to see her having fun She walked 11 steps on our first adventure and every day we went a little bit further
The healthier Nanna got the clearer it became how neglected she was her entire life She wasn't close to death because she was old, she was close to death because she never got the care she desperately needed.
The progress she made in the first year was amazing She walked around the block for the first time, then she did her first mile a few months later, at 16 years old she did her first 5k And today, on her 18th birthday -
Nanna crushed her first 10k ever ＼⁠(⁠°⁠o⁠°⁠)⁠／ The 5k at 16 years old took her 108 minutes The 10k today took her 107 minutes, it's unbelievable progress!
With all the progress it's important for me not to forget the bigger reality Nanna is 18 years old, full of comorbidities and she's going to die a hell of a lot sooner than later.
That's why it's so important for me to celebrate all of these milestones. I've really made it a point this year to take her to as many new places as possible, feed her as many new things as I can and just try to experience as much as possible before it's too late
I've never really had a dog that needed to be taken care of before, my other dogs are so independent but Nanna needs care just about every waking moment. It's brought some of my toughest days in addiction recovery, and when things go right like they did today, some of the most beautiful moments as well.
This is my 18 year old puppy Nanna Pants She deserves to have the happiest birthday in history.
With her birthday every year we kick off Puckett Week - supporting domestic violence survivors facing homelessness
The supplies Imgur sent to abuse survivors in the first year of Puckett Week couldn't even fit in the picture frame it was amazing You might be wondering why domestic violence survivors? What does that have to do with me or dogs?
I was homeless for years with a small incredible dog named Mr Puckett It was the most terrifying time of our lives.
As domestic violence contributes to homelessness for women and children more than any other cause, supporting these families is a way to keep them off the street entirely The Mary Parrish Center has beautiful apartment homes where survivors can stay safe and start their life fresh with all the support they need
The Mary Parrish Center set up an Amazon Wishlist where you can send household items to abuse survivors staying in their safe apartments When residents are back on their feet and ready to move out, they even get to take everything in the furnished apartments to make the transition easier
Or if you'd like to donate $18 in honor of Nanna's 18th birthday I'll include the link to Puckett Week on their website below. It's a cause and an organization that means a lot to me and I'm always hopeful it means a lot to everyone else as well.
I don't know if this will be Nanna's last birthday ever, but I'm sure as hell going to treat her as if it is Every food she wants, every adventure she wants...
Anything to pay her back for all the love and joy she's brought into my life Happy birthday Nanna Pants, we all love you so goddamn much. ... To donate $18 to abuse survivors in honor of Nanna's 18th birthday - https://www.maryparrish.org/donate ... If you'd like to send supplies to the survivors of domestic violence at Mary Parrish - https://www.amazon.com/hz/wishlist/ls/3USHUHIVSINQE?ref_=wl_share ... Thank you so much for reading my post, more on the other Pucketts later this week!
item.dc:creator: MrPuckett

item.title: 15th Favorite
item.description: I've enjoyed how Terry Crews has been true to himself. Honesty is one of the greatest traits people have. I also thoroughly enjoy lego. It is one of the most therapeutic things I've found in sobriety. Anxiety and depression suck donkey balls, but giving up and letting those douche nozzles win isn't an option. One step at a time and I've gotten further than I've ever expected. Yep, setbacks happen and it'd be weird if they didn't. Anywhoozle, have a great night and rant over.
item.dc:creator: DarthInsomnias

item.title: Cake for Imgur's (My Husband's) Bday
item.description:
I bought a mini bundt cake to celebrate my husband's birthday. Yup - I'm Imgur's wife... It's this whole ongoing saga: https://imgur.com/gallery/9NkiCni
I ate it in front of my laptop with Imgur open so they could watch... Is this post weird enough yet? I feel like Upvote Everything Day is the only time I get to be as weird as I want to be on here so I had to grab this opportunity :P
item.dc:creator: Alsoashley

item.title: Time to Enjoy

Mercury through cloth
You scared me!
Use your skills!
Enjoy, we're partying
Follow the team
Run as much as you can
The End

item.title: This reply to a picture of a girl asleep who's had her period and someone asking "fellas what would you do if you go home and see this" is fucking perfect!
item.description: Edit: Someone commented asking why would I post this, not an aggressive comment, just asking... The answer is because I'm a woman, more men need to understand it's normal, it happens and have tips in how to handle it, imo

item.title: Fist day off work. Happy 15 yrs Imgur!
item.description: Just sitting here with my Hot tea watching some YouTube and I pick up the phone to check some posts. The McDonaldo's ad are getting annoying and just to see Imgur's birthday thingy. Cool :). I wanted to check any post I liked so I can share. . . But ehh. Too many to choose from so. I pic, a post, Hashing. And another sip. Ahh. . . Happy Birthday Imgur. Happing posting everybody.
